title: ReleaseResult
description: Output of bin/release.sh — generate CHANGELOG section, commit, tag. Consumed by skills/release/SKILL.md.

from.description: Reference (tag or SHA) the changelog was generated from.

changelog.description: The rendered Markdown CHANGELOG block that was prepended.

pushed.description: True only when --push was requested AND every push step (tag, plus the conventional-changelog branch push) succeeded. False otherwise — including when --push wasn't requested at all. The exit code is non-zero when --push was requested but pushed is false; consumers should also inspect next_steps[].

next_steps.description: Concrete recovery commands the user should run when a push step failed mid-release. Empty when nothing failed. Each entry is a copy-paste-ready shell snippet.

dry_run.description: True when emitted from the --dry-run path (no commit/tag/push performed). Absent on a real release run. Consumers can use this to gate any side-effect that should only fire on the real path.

prerelease.description: True when --version contains a SemVer pre-release suffix (e.g. `1.0.0-rc.1`, `2.0.0-alpha`). Prerelease cuts skip CHANGELOG promotion (the [Unreleased] section stays queued for the eventual stable release) and skip the release-commit branch push. Consumers should pass `--prerelease` to `gh release create` when this is true.

ci_gate.description: Present only when --wait-for-checks was passed and the run wasn't --dry-run AND the gate let the release proceed. Failed gates (CI fail / timeout / unreachable gh / no-PR-found-without-opt-in) die before any JSON is emitted, so the only outcomes that surface here are the ones that allowed the tag to be created.

outcome.description: pass = polled PR's checks all green; no-checks = polled PR had no checks attached; no-pr-found = no PR matched HEAD's SHA AND the user passed --allow-no-pr to opt out of the gate (legitimate for first-cut releases or local-only commits — without --allow-no-pr the no-PR case dies hard to prevent silent CI bypass on squash/rebase release flows).

pr_number.description: PR number that was polled. Absent when outcome is 'no-pr-found'.

oneOf.title: ReleaseSkipped
oneOf.description: Emitted when --strategy is 'changesets' or 'release-please' (nyann doesn't duplicate those tools).

oneOf.title: ReleaseNoop
oneOf.description: Emitted when no commits exist between --from and HEAD (nothing to release).
